stem: A web performance study measures the response time (ms) for a sample of 25 website users. The sample mean is 229.9 ms with a sample standard deviation of 65.8 ms. However, 2 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 23 observations have a mean of 213.1 ms and standard deviation of 43.8 ms. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 229.9, 95% CI = [202.7, 257.1]. Without outliers: mean = 213.1, 95% CI = [194.8, 231.4]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [194.8, 257.1]. Point estimate: 221.5 ms.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [194.8, 257.1].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
